description:
Assume you are a QA engineer who has just received a tiny test-payload that you must
    place under checksum control before the rest of the automated suite is allowed to
    touch it.

    1. The payload already exists at:
       /home/user/sample_data/testfile.txt

    2. Create the directory in which all checksum evidence will live:
       /home/user/logs

    3. Using the SHA-256 algorithm, generate a checksum for the payload and write a
       two-line log file called /home/user/logs/pkg_checksum.log that follows this
       *exact* format (including the trailing newline after each line and the absence of
       extra spaces):  

       FILENAME: testfile.txt  
       SHA256: <64-hex-digits>  

       Example of the required file structure (do **not** hard-code these values—this
       is only to show spacing and newlines):

       FILENAME: somefile.bin  
       SHA256: 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef  

       Important formatting rules the automated tests will enforce:
       • There must be **exactly** two lines.  
       • The first line must begin with the literal string “FILENAME: ” (note the
         single space after the colon) followed immediately by the base name of the
         file (not its path).  
       • The second line must begin with the literal string “SHA256: ” (note the
         single space after the colon) followed immediately by the 64-character,
         all-lower-case hexadecimal digest.  
       • Each line must end with a single Unix newline (LF, \n).  
       • No other characters, spaces, or blank lines are allowed before, between, or
         after the two lines.  

    4. When finished, you can cat the log file to the terminal so that the calling
       system (and a human reviewer) can quickly see your results.

    Your job is complete when:
       • /home/user/logs/pkg_checksum.log exists.  
       • The file obeys the exact format above.  
       • The SHA-256 digest inside the log matches the actual checksum of
         /home/user/sample_data/testfile.txt.

    Hint: you will likely need only 2–3 commands total: one to make the directory, one
    to calculate the checksum, and one to create/write the log file.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing file needed for the task
    -->
    File: /home/user/sample_data/testfile.txt
    Permissions: 0644
    Content (exact, 1 byte, no newline): a

    <!--
        Expected SHA-256 checksum of the payload with NO trailing newline
    -->
    Expected checksum (lower-case hex, 64 chars):
    ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb

    <!--
        Expected final log file
    -->
    Expected file created by the agent: /home/user/logs/pkg_checksum.log
    Permissions after creation: 0644 (any user-writable default is acceptable)
    Expected contents (including final newline on each line):
    FILENAME: testfile.txt\n
    SHA256: ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb\n
name: task_000000_8afdd2f9
